title: Why Valhi (VHI) Sank Into the Red as Its Chemicals Business Unraveled
content:
Valhi, Inc. (NYSE:VHI) is one of the best short-term stocks to buy now. On March 10, 2026, the company reported a net loss attributable to stockholders of $53.2 million, or $1.86 per share, for the fourth quarter of 2025, compared with net income of $22.8 million, or $0.80 per share, in the year-earlier quarter. For full-year 2025, Valhi posted a net loss of $57.6 million, or $2.02 per share, versus net income of $108.0 million, or $3.79 per share, in 2024. Management said the decline was driven primarily by weaker operating results in its Chemicals segment.

That business remained the main drag. Chemicals segment net sales slipped 1% to $418.3 million in the fourth quarter and 1% to $1.9 billion for the full year. The segment swung to a $60.1 million operating loss in the quarter from operating income of $32.6 million a year earlier, as lower TiO2 selling prices, reduced operating rates, and about $10.3 million of workforce-reduction costs hit results.Why Valhi (VHI) Sank Into the Red as Its Chemicals Business Unraveled

For the full year, the segment recorded a $24.5 million operating loss versus $138.5 million of operating income in 2024. Valhi also said fourth-quarter results included an $8.5 million non-cash deferred income tax expense tied to a valuation allowance in the Chemicals segment’s German deferred tax asset.

Valhi, Inc. (NYSE: VHI) operates in chemicals, component products, and real estate management and development.

While we acknowledge the potential of VHI as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: TSX Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In April 2026
content:
As Canadian markets show signs of recovery, with the TSX index posting positive returns after a challenging March, investors are closely monitoring the ongoing oil price fluctuations and their potential impact on economic growth. In such uncertain times, companies with high insider ownership can be appealing to investors seeking alignment between management and shareholder interests; this article highlights three growth-oriented stocks on the TSX where insiders hold significant stakes.

Top 10 Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In Canada

Click here to see the full list of 44 stocks from our Fast Growing TSX Companies With High Insider Ownership screener.

Below we spotlight a couple of our favorites from our exclusive screener.

Aduro Clean Technologies

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Aduro Clean Technologies Inc. focuses on developing water-based chemical recycling technologies and has a market cap of CA$479.62 million.

Operations: The company's revenue segment includes CA$0.31 million from Pollution and Treatment Control Products.

Insider Ownership: 33.9%

Revenue Growth Forecast: 73% p.a.

Aduro Clean Technologies is advancing its Hydrochemolytic Technology with a focus on chemical recycling, highlighted by joining Chemical Recycling Europe and selecting Chemelot Industrial Park for its first-of-a-kind plant. Despite limited current revenue (CA$0.31 million), forecasts suggest significant annual growth in both revenue (73%) and profitability over the next three years, surpassing market averages. Recent strategic alliances and site developments enhance Aduro's positioning in the circular economy, though insider trading activity remains minimal.

Dive into the specifics of Aduro Clean Technologies here with our thorough growth forecast report. In light of our recent valuation report, it seems possible that Aduro Clean Technologies is trading beyond its estimated value.CNSX:ACT Ownership Breakdown as at Apr 2026

Propel Holdings

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Propel Holdings Inc., along with its subsidiaries, operates as a financial technology company with a market cap of CA$725.08 million.

Operations: The company generates revenue of $589.81 million by offering lending-related services to borrowers, banks, and other institutions.

Story Continues

Insider Ownership: 29.7%

Revenue Growth Forecast: 22.3% p.a.

Propel Holdings is leveraging its AI-powered platform to drive growth, evidenced by the launch of FreshLine with a US$150 million commitment. Despite a recent dip in quarterly net income to US$5.94 million, annual revenue rose significantly to US$589.81 million. Forecasts suggest Propel's earnings and revenue will grow substantially faster than the Canadian market over the next three years. However, insider trading has shown more selling than buying recently, and dividends are not well covered by free cash flow.

Navigate through the intricacies of Propel Holdings with our comprehensive analyst estimates report here. Our expertly prepared valuation report Propel Holdings implies its share price may be lower than expected.TSX:PRL Ownership Breakdown as at Apr 2026

Vitalhub

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Vitalhub Corp. offers technology and software solutions for health and human service providers across Canada, the United States, the United Kingdom, Australia, Western Asia, and other international markets with a market cap of CA$436.30 million.

Operations: The company's revenue from healthcare software solutions amounts to CA$108.97 million.

Insider Ownership: 11%

Revenue Growth Forecast: 12.1% p.a.

Vitalhub is experiencing significant earnings growth, projected at 37.98% annually, outpacing the Canadian market's 10.3%. Despite a forecasted revenue growth of 12.1%, slower than the ideal 20%, it surpasses the market average of 5.2%. Recent financials reveal a jump in annual revenue to C$108.97 million and net income doubling to C$6.11 million year-over-year, with insiders buying more shares than selling recently, albeit not substantially so.
